vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base, or column slot on bench-seat trims
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Cap at side of console shifter base, or column slot on bench-seat trims.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case MUST be in 2H before towing on road.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever (older) or Electric push-button on dash (newer) — labeled 2H / 4A / 4H / 4L
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4A — automatic 4WD (if equipped) — NOT safe for towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: MANUAL LEVER: Stop completely. Move TC floor lever firmly to 2H.
ELECTRIC PUSH-BUTTON: Slow to under 3 MPH. Press the 2H button on the dash.
Wait for the 2H indicator lamp to illuminate on the instrument cluster.
Confirm all 4WD indicator lights are OFF before lifting rear wheels.
NEVER tow in 4H or 4L — serious driveline damage will result.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard red (+) / black (−) terminals
battery.warnings: Confirm TC in 2WD before wheel lift.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.warn: Lightning EV: FLATBED ONLY. 4WD: transfer case.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4A — automatic 4WD — NOT safe for towing
4H — part-time 4WD high — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn the rotary TC dial on the center console to 2H.
Confirm the 2H indicator lamp on the instrument cluster.
Verify all 4WD lights are OFF before lifting rear wheels.
PowerBoost Hybrid: same TC procedure. F-150 Lightning EV: FLATBED ONLY — no TC.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.warnings: PowerBoost Hybrid: 12V only for jumping. Silverado EV: 12V in frunk.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the F-150 Lightning to ON/Ready mode (main power on — not a start).
Shift lock slot: Trim panel near the electronic gear selector — remove to expose slot.
Remove the trim panel using a trim pry tool or fingernail at panel edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the electronic selector to Neutral.
FLATBED ONLY — dual electric motors on all Lightning models cannot be disabled.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

FRUNK BATTERY: Open the front hood — there is no engine. The 12V auxiliary battery is in the front storage compartment (frunk). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the HV battery. Connect jump pack to 12V frunk terminals.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
VERY HEAVY VEHICLE (~6,000–6,500 lbs) — verify flatbed is rated for this weight before loading.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.notes: HV battery in frame — never touch.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain, no transfer case
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery in Frunk — Not Under Hood
knownIssues.detail: The F-150 Lightning has a front trunk (frunk) where the 12V auxiliary battery is located. The front hood opens but there is no conventional engine — the frunk IS the storage area. Pop the hood, and the battery is in the front storage compartment.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — Electric Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: The Lightning has dual electric motors driving all four wheels. Towing with any wheels on the ground will damage the motors. No exceptions — flatbed only.

knownIssues.title: Very Heavy Vehicle
knownIssues.detail: The F-150 Lightning weighs approximately 6,000-6,500 lbs depending on battery size. Verify your flatbed is rated for this weight. Many standard flatbeds are rated for 5,000 lbs and will be over capacity.

battery.location: FRUNK (front trunk) — open the hood, battery is in the front storage area
battery.jumpTerminals: Inside the frunk — red (+) / black (−) terminals on auxiliary 12V battery
battery.warnings: No engine bay — the frunk IS the front compartment when you open the hood. NEVER touch HV battery. Very heavy vehicle (~6,000-6,500 lbs).

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side. Diesel trims may have dual batteries..
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.notes: Power Stroke diesel: same procedure. 4WD standard.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case MUST be in 2H. Heavy-duty — heavy blocks required.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Heavy-duty — use rated equipment. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (TouchDrive) or manual floor lever depending on year
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: ELECTRIC (TouchDrive): Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H button on dash. Confirm 2H indicator.
MANUAL LEVER: Stop completely. Move TC lever to 2H.
Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
Heavy-duty truck — use properly rated chains, straps, and wheel blocks.
Do NOT tow in 4H or 4L on pavement.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.location: Under hood, driver side. Diesel trims may have dual batteries.
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red (+) on either battery, black (−) to engine block or frame
battery.warnings: Diesel: two batteries in parallel. Jump to either positive terminal. Heavy vehicle.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Diesel Power Stroke may have dual batteries..
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Power Stroke diesel: same. Tremor: same.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Heavy-duty — use rated equipment. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial (electronic TC shift) on center console
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4A — automatic 4WD (if equipped) — NOT safe for towing
4H — part-time 4WD high — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn rotary TC dial to 2H.
Confirm 2H indicator on instrument cluster.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are OFF before lifting.
Do NOT tow in 4H or 4L on pavement.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.location: Under hood. Diesel Power Stroke may have dual batteries.
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Diesel: either positive terminal works.
battery.warnings: Diesel dual battery: jump to either positive terminal. Very heavy.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD/AWD: transfer case to 2H or flatbed. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric (Control-Trac) or manual lever depending on model and generation
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD engagement — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: CONTROL-TRAC (electric): Slow under 3 MPH. Use 4WD switch to select 2WD. Confirm indicator.
MANUAL LEVER: Stop completely. Shift TC lever to 2WD.
Confirm 2WD indicator before lifting rear wheels.
AWD-only models (no 2WD mode): FLATBED ONLY.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
AWD VARIANT: Use flatbed. If no flatbed, wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.

vehicles.slot: Column shifter — slot on the steering column
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Column shifter — slot on the steering column.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2H button on the dash.
Confirm 2H indicator on instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Column Shifter — Slot on Steering Column
knownIssues.detail: These Expeditions use a column-mounted shifter, not a console shifter. The shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not on the center console. Operators looking on the floor console will not find it.

battery.warnings: Column shifter — slot is on steering column, not console.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base under trim panel on console.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case. Large SUV — heavy blocks.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4A — automatic 4WD — NOT safe for towing
4H — part-time 4WD high
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Turn rotary dial to 2H.
Confirm 2H indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels once in 2H.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (2020+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (2020+).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. Escape PHEV: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid/PHEV: 12V only.
battery.warnings: Escape PHEV: 12V only — never touch HV battery.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (newer).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Under the SHIFTER BOOT material — insert flathead between boot and trim ring, lift boot to expose lever
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Under the SHIFTER BOOT material — insert flathead between boot and trim ring, lift boot to expose lever.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Under the SHIFTER BOOT material — insert flathead between boot and trim ring, lift boot to expose lever.
vehicles.notes: Ford Brake Shift Interlock (BSI) system. 2-door and 4-door: same.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case in 2H or 4H (NOT 4LO) before towing.
vehicles.towMethod: Transfer case to 2H first (if equipped), then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Base models may not have 2H — flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever — 2-speed Command-Trac (base/Black Diamond/Big Bend) or Rock-Trac (Badlands/Raptor/Wildtrak)
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing (Raptor and most non-base trims have 2H)
4H — part-time 4WD high range — under 55 MPH on loose surfaces only
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: IMPORTANT: Some base Bronco trims do NOT have a 2H position — must use flatbed.
For 2H-equipped models: Stop completely or slow under 3 MPH.
Move the TC floor lever to the 2H position.
Confirm 2H indicator on the instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed in 2H.
For flat towing a Bronco: see full procedure in owner's manual — requires specific TC and transmission positions.
NEVER tow in 4L.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.warnings: Some base trims have no 2H position — flatbed required if no 2WD mode.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side near firewall.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: Under hood, driver side near firewall

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Mach-E to ON/Ready mode.
The Mach-E uses an ELECTRONIC ROTARY DIAL selector on the center console.
Shift lock slot: Electronic rotary selector — slot near base of the dial.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot at the dial base.
Press firmly and rotate the dial toward N.
FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain, driven wheels must not contact the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

FRUNK BATTERY: Open the front hood — there is no engine. The 12V auxiliary battery is in the front storage compartment (frunk). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the HV battery. Connect jump pack to 12V terminals.
Once 12V power is restored, rotate dial to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery in Frunk
knownIssues.detail: The Mach-E 12V auxiliary battery is in the frunk (front trunk), not under the hood in a conventional sense. Open the hood — the battery is in the front storage compartment with accessible jump terminals.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY
knownIssues.detail: Electric AWD or RWD drivetrain. Towing with wheels on the ground will damage the motors. Flatbed only with no exceptions.

battery.location: FRUNK — open front hood, battery is in the front storage compartment
battery.jumpTerminals: Inside the frunk — 12V auxiliary red (+) / black (−)
battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot under trim near selector.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2H button on the center console.
Confirm the 2H indicator illuminates.
Safe to lift rear wheels once in 2H.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic shifter (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or slot near electronic shifter (newer).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/PHEV: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid/PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DCT dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Cap near console shifter base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DCT transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: Focus Electric: flatbed only. PowerShift DCT: restore power preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

vehicles.slot: COLUMN SHIFTER — slot on the steering column
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — COLUMN SHIFTER — slot on the steering column.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.warn: Large van — heavy wheel blocks required.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.

battery.warnings: Large van — heavy wheel blocks required.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.
